markets.question: Will T1 make a roster change before July?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player joins or leaves T1's active League of Legends roster (defined as the active lineup listed on the T1 Liquipedia page: https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/T1) by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

As of market creation the active roster consists of the following players: Doran, Oner, Faker, Peyz, and Keria. Name changes or aliases referring to the same player do not qualify as roster changes.

For the purpose of this market, "roster change" refers to any official signing, transfer, benching to inactive/reserve, release, retirement, or departure of a player from the active lineup. This includes adding new players (transfers, free agent signings, or loans), removing players (to inactive, free agency, or other teams), or any change resulting in a different active roster.

Coach changes and temporary stand-ins for a single match will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be Liquipedia; however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a dead-even field for pole position at the Canadian Grand Prix, with all 22 drivers priced at 50% implied probability amid 2026's transformative active aero and sustainable power unit regulations that have compressed one-lap pace across the grid. Early-season qualifying has featured razor-thin margins, highlighted by rookie sensation Kimi Antonelli's perfect 100% win rate from pole in limited starts and strong average finishes near the front, alongside consistent top-end showings from Oscar Piastri, Max Verstappen, and George Russell. Circuit Gilles Villeneuve's low-downforce demands for straight-line speed and braking stability favor versatile setups, but with no practice sessions yet and over three weeks until the May 22-24 weekend, no clear qualifying favorite has emerged to shift sentiment.

markets.question: Will Lars Boje Mathiesen be the next prime minister of Denmark after the 2026 parliamentary elections?

Coalition negotiations assigned to Frederiksen as initial formateur stalled amid disagreements with centrist Moderates leader Lars Løkke Rasmussen, culminating in a May 8 breakdown when a key partner withdrew; the king has now tasked Venstre leader and Defence Minister Troels Lund Poulsen (12.1% odds) to explore a centre-right government. Recent polls show Social Democrats steady at 21% amid opposition fragmentation, bolstering her position for a potential minority or broad coalition ahead of 2030 polls.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Moscow be 10°C on May 9?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by NOAA at the Vnukovo International Airport in degrees Celsius on 9 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from NOAA, specifically the highest reading under the "Temp" column on the specified date once information is finalized for all hours on that date, available here: https://www.weather.gov/wrh/timeseries?site=UUWW

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the "Switch to Metric Units" button until the relevant table displays °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

Persistent low-level stratus clouds and light precipitation potential suppress solar heating, capping maxima well below the climatological May average of 18°C; ECMWF runs lean slightly cooler at 12°C due to thicker overcast, while GFS implies 13°C with marginally better mixing. Differentiation hinges on subtle model discrepancies in cloud timing and precipitation intensity—intensified rain could nudge toward 11°C. Watch final 00Z model updates overnight and Vnukovo Airport observations for resolution cues.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

The pair has rebounded from YTD lows near 1.1435 in March, reflecting trader bets on Fed rate cuts narrowing policy divergence. Forecasts cluster around 1.20 by year-end 2026, with June 16-17 FOMC and ECB July meetings as pivotal catalysts.

markets.question: Will LeBron James buy the Seattle Seahawks?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the named individual who is publicly announced as having agreed to purchase the Seattle Seahawks by September 9, 2026, at 11:59 PM ET (the beginning of the 2026 NFL regular season). 

If no sale is announced before this time, this market will resolve to "Other".

For the purposes of this market, an announcement must confirm that the Paul G. Allen estate (also known as Vulcan LLC) has reached a binding agreement to sell a majority interest in the team. Minority sales will not be considered.

If a sale is announced to an individual that is not listed, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If two or more listed individuals are announced as joint owners or part of the same ownership group, this market will resolve to the individual announced as the controlling owner.

If two or more listed individuals are announced as the controlling owners, this market will resolve to the individual whose last name comes first alphabetically.

For the purposes of this market, the completion (closing) of the sale is not required.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the National Football League or the Seattle Seahawks. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

The Paul G. markets.question: Will the median home value in the DC Metro area be between $566,000 and $572,000 on May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the median home value for all property types in the Washington, D.C. Metro area on May 31, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source will be official data from the Parcl Labs Sales Price Index for the Washington, D.C. Metro area (Parcl_ID: 2900475). The settlement price will be calculated by multiplying the published price index value (price per square foot) by 1800 square feet, which is the median home size in the Washington, D.C. Metro area. Parcl is set to publish this data on May 31, 2026. If no data for May 31 is released by June 10, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve according to the most recently published data. (see: https://app.parcllabs.com/prediction-market-resolutions/36)

eventMetadata.context_description: World No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka enters as a heavy favorite at the Internazionali BNL d'Italia after a dominant 6-2, 6-3 second-round win over Barbora Krejcikova on clay, showcasing her explosive baseline power and improved movement on the surface following a strong Madrid run marred only by a quarterfinal loss to Hailey Baptiste. Sorana Cirstea, ranked around No. 26 and on her farewell 2026 tour, advanced comfortably 6-2, 6-0 over Tatjana Maria despite a recent leg injury withdrawal in Rouen, but trails 2-1 in head-to-heads, including a straight-sets defeat to Sabalenka in Brisbane earlier this year. Trader consensus reflects Sabalenka's ranking edge, youth, and stylistic matchup superiority on Rome's slower clay, though Cirstea's veteran experience could extend rallies.

In the upcoming Serie B game, scheduled for May 8 at 2:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "SS Juve Stabia" if SS Juve Stabia win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "FC Südtirol".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on legaserieb.it. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: FC Südtirol vs. SS Juve Stabia - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Serie B game, scheduled for May 8 at 2:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the late Serie B season with promotion playoffs looming, 7th-placed SS Juve Stabia hold a slight edge over 11th-placed FC Südtirol, who sit mid-table but mired in a seven-match winless streak including recent draws against Modena and Cesena plus losses to Spezia (6-1) and Frosinone. Südtirol host at Stadio Druso, where they won the reverse fixture 1-0 in December 2025, though Juve Stabia lead the head-to-head 2-1 overall; trader consensus reflects Juve Stabia's stronger recent form (DW LWD) and neutral goal difference versus Südtirol's -9. Key absences include Südtirol's goalkeeper Alessio Cragno (Achilles, out until late May) and suspended Karim Zedadka, plus Juve Stabia's Leonardo Candellone (muscle) and Gregorio Morachioli (fitness concerns), with mild 20°C weather forecast.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest National Weather Service guidance for Seattle-Tacoma International Airport (KSEA) projects a high near 66°F on May 10 under partly sunny conditions with light onshore flow, driving trader consensus toward the closely matched 64-65°F (27.5%) and 66-67°F (26.5%) outcomes. This reflects model agreement between GFS and ECMWF ensembles on a weak upper-level ridge fostering above-climatological temperatures—May 10 averages 64°F—but moderated by persistent Pacific marine layer stratus, which could limit mixing and cap peaks at 65°F if slow to burn off. Recent developments include a warming trend through early May, with highs reaching low 70s amid record challenges, though strengthening northwest winds introduce uncertainty; full afternoon sunshine favors 67-69°F. New 12Z model runs and NWS updates expected Saturday morning could shift probabilities further.

markets.question: Will Movimento Democrático Brasileiro (MDB) win the most seats in the next Brazilian Senate election?
markets.description:
The next federal Senate election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, in which two-thirds of the Senate’s 81 seats will be contested.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

events.title: Next Brazil Senate Election: Most Seats Won
events.description:
The next federal Senate election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, in which two-thirds of the Senate’s 81 seats will be contested.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Brazilian Senate, not by any federation or coalition it may be a part of.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors the PL at 81.5% implied probability to win the most seats in Brazil's October 4, 2026, Senate election, where 54 positions are up for renewal, driven by the party's strong showings in recent state-level polls and aggressive candidate launches. Late April-early May Datatrends, Verita, and Simetria surveys highlight PL contenders like André do Prado in São Paulo—endorsed by Eduardo Bolsonaro and predicted by Governor Tarcísio de Freitas to lead votes—and competitive positioning in Bahia, Pará, and the Midwest, building on PL's current status as the Senate's largest party. The MDB (5.1%) benefits from centrists like Helder Barbalho but lags in projections; UNIÃO and PSD trail amid fragmented opposition. Senate rejection of Lula's Supreme Court nominee on April 29 further energized right-wing bets, though coalition negotiations and fresh polls ahead of primaries remain pivotal.

markets.question: Series: Anyone's Legend Kill Handicap (-10.5) vs Weibo Gaming (+10.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Anyone's Legend and Weibo Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, scheduled for January 20 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend secures 11 or more kills than Weibo Gaming in the match. 

Otherwise, it will resolve to "Weibo Gaming".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anyone's Legend enters this LPL Group Ascend BO3 as a top-four contender at 7-4 overall, holding a +5 game differential after a momentum-building 2-0 sweep over Ninjas in Pyjamas on May 7, which solidified their direct playoff push in the double round-robin format. Weibo Gaming, mired at 4-8 and seventh place with a -6 differential, showed upset potential by edging AL 2-1 on May 3 but stumbled 0-2 to Team WE the next week, highlighting inconsistent form amid Hard Fearless Draft pressures. Head-to-head splits evenly across recent series, with AL's Tarzan earning multiple MVPs via jungle dominance contrasting WBG's reliance on Xiaohu mid-lane carries; stable rosters and remaining schedule intensity could amplify stylistic clashes in laning and macro execution.

events.title: NBA Playoffs: Eastern Conference Champion
events.description: This is a market on which team will win the Eastern Conference Finals in the 2025–26 NBA season.

eventMetadata.context_description: Detroit Pistons and New York Knicks dominate trader consensus as Eastern Conference favorites after surging to 2-0 leads in the conference semifinals, with Cade Cunningham's 25 points and 10 assists powering Detroit's 107-97 Game 2 win over Cleveland Cavaliers on May 7, following a 111-101 opener. Jalen Brunson added 26 points to New York’s 108-102 Game 2 victory versus Philadelphia 76ers on May 6, after a 137-98 rout in Game 1, bolstering Knicks' edge amid OG Anunoby's questionable hamstring status for Game 3. Joel Embiid's hip/ankle issues sidelined him in Game 2, dimming 76ers' upset hopes despite their first-round Celtics triumph, while Cavaliers face momentum challenges down 0-2. Close odds reflect both leaders' strong form and paths to the conference finals.

markets.question: Decibel FDV above $200M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Decibel's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Decibel (https://x.com/DecibelTrade) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

Carlos Alcaraz trails at 19% due to a persistent right wrist injury forcing French Open withdrawal and late-clay absences, clouding his title defense prospects despite strong grass history. Novak Djokovic's 9.2% implied probability stems from his shock Round of 64 Italian Open exit to qualifier Dino Prizmic—his first match since a two-month injury layoff—highlighting physical struggles at age 39. Zverev (3.9%) and British hopeful Jack Draper (3.5%) gain traction with top-10 form and surface suitability post-Roland Garros.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a tight contest among 23°C (33.5%), 25°C (26.5%), and 24°C (22.5%) for Chongqing's highest temperature on May 9 at Jiangbei International Airport station, reflecting model ensembles from the China Meteorological Administration (CMA), ECMWF, and GFS showing highs clustered in the low-to-mid 20s Celsius amid persistent cloud cover. Yesterday's unusually cool 19°C high—driven by overcast skies and light winds—has tempered expectations, with forecasts indicating partial clearing but limited insolation potential due to mid-level troughing over the Sichuan Basin. Key differentiators include afternoon cloud break timing and boundary layer mixing, with CMA guidance at 24°C but ensemble spreads of 22-26°C highlighting short-range uncertainty; updated model runs expected overnight could shift odds further. Historical early-May averages near 26°C provide context, though current synoptic patterns favor moderation.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the TX-38 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the TX-38 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

events.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the TX-38 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: The TX-38 House seat, an open race after incumbent Wesley Hunt's U.S. Senate bid, favors Republicans at 81.5% trader consensus due to the district's entrenched R+12 Cook PVI and consistent 25-point GOP margins in 2022 and 2024 general elections. Democratic nominee Melissa McDonough, who lost 37%-63% to Hunt last cycle, faces a steep uphill battle in the Houston-area suburbs. On the GOP side, Trump-endorsed mortgage banker Jon Bonck, the top primary vote-getter at 47.7% on March 3, heads to the May 26 runoff against Shelly deZevallos; forecasters like Cook Political rate it Solid Republican. No major developments in the past 30 days have shifted the dynamics ahead of the November 3 general election.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rev. Adam Hamilton's late entry into the Kansas Democratic U.S. Senate primary on April 30 has propelled him to overwhelming trader consensus at 78.5%, fueled by his profile as founding pastor of the nation's largest United Methodist church and over $1 million raised in his campaign's first week. This surge overshadows earlier frontrunners like state Sen. Patrick Schmidt at 11.4%, who led first-quarter fundraising among Democrats but now trails amid Hamilton's media dominance and centrist appeal in a crowded field of over 10 candidates. State Sen. Sandy Spidel Neumann holds third at 4.9% on limited visibility, while others lag due to weaker name recognition and resources. With the August 4 primary approaching, traders price Hamilton's momentum from his Kansas listening tour and church network as key to consolidating moderate voters, though shifts could arise from endorsements or debates.

markets.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for May 8 at 9:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Stephon Castle records more than 5.5 total rebounds during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if Stephon Castle records 5.5 rebounds or fewer during the game.

The entire game including all overtime periods will be considered.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not take the court at any point, the market will resolve "No".

The resolution source will be the official NBA box score as published on NBA.com.

events.title: Spurs vs. Timberwolves
events.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for May 8 at 12:00AM ET:
If the Spurs win, the market will resolve to "Spurs".
If the Timberwolves win, the market will resolve to "Timberwolves".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: In Game 3 of the Western Conference semifinals at Target Center, the San Antonio Spurs hold a 2-0 series lead over the Minnesota Timberwolves following a dominant 133-95 Game 2 road win for San Antonio, fueled by strong performances from Victor Wembanyama and De'Aaron Fox amid Minnesota's shooting woes. Timberwolves star Anthony Edwards remains questionable with a left knee bone bruise that has lingered since the first round, while Ayo Dosunmu (right calf soreness) is also questionable and Donte DiVincenzo is out with Achilles surgery—thinning Minnesota's backcourt depth significantly. Spurs report no major injuries, entering with momentum, rest advantage from the prior series, and a historical edge in recent head-to-heads (winning both playoff games convincingly). Home-court boost and desperation could spark a Wolves response, but trader consensus reflects San Antonio's health and form as key edges in this must-win for Minnesota.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NY-21 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-21 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: The open NY-21 House seat, vacated by Rep. Elise Stefanik's December 2025 decision to end her gubernatorial bid and retire from Congress, drives trader consensus toward a Republican hold at 65.5% implied probability, reflecting the district's deep-red upstate New York North Country profile where Donald Trump won overwhelmingly in 2024. Recent dueling polls from early May underscore a competitive GOP primary led by Anthony Constantino, whose lead widened after President Trump's April 21 endorsement bucked the state party's March backing of Robert Smullen, ahead of the June 23 primaries. Democrats field weaker challengers with no standout polling, aligning with historical R+12 partisan lean and incumbency-free Republican advantages in safe seats.

markets.question: Will Arvid Lindblad be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

events.title: F1 Drivers' Champion
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' early-season dominance under 2026 regulations has propelled Kimi Antonelli to the top of trader consensus at 38.8% implied probability for the Drivers' Championship, following his third consecutive victory at the Miami Grand Prix where he converted pole to win ahead of teammate George Russell in P4. Antonelli leads the standings with 100 points to Russell's 80 after four rounds, including his maiden China triumph and strong Australia/Japan results, highlighting Mercedes' superior race pace and energy management. Russell's consistent podiums keep him close at 29.5%, but recent qualifying edges favor the 19-year-old Italian. Trailing challengers like Lando Norris (10.9%) and Max Verstappen (6.3%) reflect Red Bull's struggles with poor starts and car balance, alongside McLaren and Ferrari's inconsistent form.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Democrat George Latimer holds a commanding position in New York's 16th Congressional District, a Cook PVI D+18 stronghold where Kamala Harris won 66% in the 2024 presidential race, reflecting trader consensus at 94.5% for the Democratic Party ahead of the November 3 general election. Latimer's 71% landslide victory over Republican Miriam Flisser in 2024, coupled with superior fundraising ($134,000 cash on hand as of late March versus zero for GOP presumptive nominee Joseph Cinquemani), solidifies the race as Solid Democratic per Cook Political Report, Inside Elections, and Sabato's Crystal Ball. With no Democratic primary challengers filed by the April deadline and weak GOP opposition, markets price in minimal upset risk. Potential shifts could arise from a late scandal, health issue for Latimer, or extraordinary Republican national midterm tailwinds, though historical base rates for such safe seats favor continuity.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Democrat Janelle Bynum's bid for reelection in Oregon's 5th Congressional District has solidified trader consensus at 82.5% for the Democratic Party, bolstered by her strong fundraising, endorsements from EMILYs List and pro-Israel groups, and a district that delivered solid support for Kamala Harris in 2024. Recent Cook Political Report upgrades to Likely Democrat reflect Republicans' recruitment struggles, with Deschutes County Commissioner Patti Adair and challenger Jonathan Lockwood as the leading GOP primary contenders lacking broader appeal in this former battleground. The May 19 primary looms as the next catalyst, but historical incumbency advantages and weak opposition sustain high Democratic implied probabilities amid limited polling.

markets.question: Will Czechia win on 2026-06-11?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for June 11, 2026
If Czechia wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Korea Republic vs. Czechia
events.description: This event is for the upcoming FIFA World Cup game, scheduled for Thursday, June 11, 2026 between Korea Republic and Czechia.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Korea Republic a slim 34.5% favorite over Czechia at 34.0% with draw at 31.0%, capturing the razor-thin dynamics of this FIFA World Cup Group A opener at neutral Estadio Akron in high-altitude Guadalajara. Czechia's momentum from their March playoff triumph over Denmark via penalties contrasts South Korea's back-to-back friendly losses to Ivory Coast and Austria, fostering uncertainty despite Korea's higher 25th FIFA ranking versus Czechia's 41st. Limited head-to-head history leans toward draws, while potential absences like South Korea's Hwang In-beom and Jae-Sung Lee alongside Czechia's Ladislav Krejcí add volatility to lineups heavy on stars like Son Heung-min and Tomas Soucek. Both sides eye crucial early points against a Mexico-led group.

markets.question: Will the price of Solana be less than $40 on May 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the final "Close" price of the Binance 1 minute candle for SOL/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the SOL/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/SOL_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance SOL/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

markets.question: Will Partido dos Trabalhadores (PT) win the most seats in the next Brazilian Senate election?
markets.description:
The next federal Senate election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, in which two-thirds of the Senate’s 81 seats will be contested.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

events.title: Next Brazil Senate Election: Most Seats Won
events.description:
The next federal Senate election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, in which two-thirds of the Senate’s 81 seats will be contested.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Brazilian Senate, not by any federation or coalition it may be a part of.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors the PL at 81.5% implied probability to win the most seats in Brazil's October 4, 2026, Senate election, where 54 positions are up for renewal, driven by the party's strong showings in recent state-level polls and aggressive candidate launches. Late April-early May Datatrends, Verita, and Simetria surveys highlight PL contenders like André do Prado in São Paulo—endorsed by Eduardo Bolsonaro and predicted by Governor Tarcísio de Freitas to lead votes—and competitive positioning in Bahia, Pará, and the Midwest, building on PL's current status as the Senate's largest party. The MDB (5.1%) benefits from centrists like Helder Barbalho but lags in projections; UNIÃO and PSD trail amid fragmented opposition. Senate rejection of Lula's Supreme Court nominee on April 29 further energized right-wing bets, though coalition negotiations and fresh polls ahead of primaries remain pivotal.
